key_insight: SimPhysNet predicts laser welding penetration with high accuracy by combining physics-informed self-supervised learning with few-shot classification.
engineering_relevance: The method reduces dependence on large labeled datasets, making penetration-state monitoring more practical for industrial laser welding quality control.
method: Uses a PINN-guided contrastive self-supervised framework to learn physically meaningful molten-pool and keyhole features from unlabeled images.
Applies three image augmentation tasks to improve representation robustness and generalization.
Uses prototypical-network few-shot learning to classify penetration state from only a small labeled subset.
evidence_signal: The model achieved 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset, and matched conventional supervised methods trained on the full labeled set.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the unlabeled and few-shot labeled welding images, so transfer to new materials or process settings should be validated carefully.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
